objective: Extract only the duplicated exact-length bounded TCP record read/write loop used by the accepted Float32 and fixed-width numeric sample families into one crate-private dependency-free helper, while preserving public facades, format-specific initialization, error stages, cancellation, deadlines, socket cleanup, activation capabilities, and observed bytes unchanged.
non_scope: Handshake header/framing extraction, runtime behavior changes, public API changes, String, Int64, chunks, multiple samples/channels, discovery, clocks, queues, recovery, or adapters.
Dependencies, unsafe/FFI, devices, official implementation source, private evidence, ambient activation, crate splitting, or Manifold authority.
outputs: One crate-private exact-length TCP record helper with explicit nonzero I/O slice and total-deadline inputs, caller cancellation, typed read/write/deadline/truncation outcomes, and no format or activation authority.

Float32 and fixed-width numeric runtimes delegate only their duplicated record byte transfer while retaining their existing public limits, errors, initialization, encoding/decoding, capabilities, and cleanup behavior.
Focused parity and damaged-path evidence proves exact bytes and typed facade outcomes remain unchanged and the helper cannot broaden runtime scope.

acceptance.proof: The crate-private helper completes exact bounded reads/writes and rejects cancellation, deadline, truncation, and I/O failure without owning format or activation policy.
All accepted Float32 and fixed-width initialization, caller sample bytes, timestamps, values, errors, and cleanup remain unchanged through the existing public facades.
No public facade, format, chunk, dependency, device, adapter, ambient activation, official-source, or Manifold authority is added.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the crate-private shared record-transfer boundary and exact nonclaims.
Record helper ownership, dependency direction, and retained facade authority.
Route exact-byte, damaged-path, and facade-parity checks.
Review lane and public-boundary impact; current guidance covers the private helper extraction.
Review facade-preserving extraction and authority separation; current guidance covers it.

commit_policy: Commit proposal, reviewed lifecycle, one facade-preserving helper extraction, validation, acceptance, and push preparation intentionally; preserve failures.
